mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate inversely drives equity discount rates. With the rate remaining near zero for an extended period, the discount rate compression supports equity valuations, leading to positive short-term forward returns.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are non-positive in more than 60% of weeks when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or if rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 20-day return is insignificant (p>0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below a key threshold signals a tightening labor market that compresses corporate profit margins via wage inflation, anticipating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return distribution after UNRATE < 6.5% does not have a significantly negative mean (one-tailed t-test, alpha=0.05) compared to all other periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising in conjunction with a low federal funds rate indicates strengthening housing demand supported by easy monetary policy, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving equity prices higher over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return in triggered periods is not significantly positive (p-value > 0.05) relative to a matched control sample of non-triggered periods, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession expansion phase, real GDP growth above a threshold signals that the economy has sufficient momentum to sustain corporate earnings growth, leading to positive forward returns, whereas below-threshold growth warns of slowdown.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return in months with GDP growth > 2% is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (t-test, alpha=0.05), the hypothesis is rejected. Also rejected if the sign of the relationship flips in any other identified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index increases reflect expanding manufacturing activity and aggregate supply, which correlates positively with corporate earnings and thus drives up equity prices over a short weekly horizon.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of INDPRO with forward 5-day returns is negative over any rolling 2-year window, or if the t-statistic of the predictive coefficient is below 1.65 in a regression controlling for other macro variables.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year CPI inflation exceeding a threshold of 2.5% signals overheating and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, raising discount rates and lowering equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: If forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative (one-tailed test, p<0.10) when the inflation threshold is crossed, or if the threshold does not produce a higher Sharpe ratio than a simple CPIAUCSL level factor.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payroll employment and retail sales are growing robustly, it confirms a synchronized expansion in labor income and consumer spending, which should lift corporate profits and equity prices over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 150,000 AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return after concurrent positive surprises is not significantly higher (t-test, p<0.05) than in periods with only one or neither of the conditions met, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, the federal funds rate is stuck near zero, so monetary policy is persistently accommodative. This environment suppresses short-term discount rates and supports equity valuations, implying positive forward returns over a quarterly horizon, regardless of the exact rate level.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return when FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% is not positive and statistically significant (t-test, p<0.05), or if there are extended periods with negative returns despite the zero-rate condition, the hypothesis is rejected.
